title: Modified elastic resistance band/workout band
abstract: There is provided a modified elastic resistance band that has an elongated strap with a connection member. First and second portions of the connection member are provided at opposite ends of the elongated strap, and the first and second portions are selectively separated for ease of positioning by the user at a desired location along the leg of the user, and then the first and second portions are joined together to allow the user to proceed with exercising. Another version includes multiple resistance strands that can be selectively added or removed from the assembly to provide different levels of resistance as required by the user. The individual strands are joined at opposite ends to the first and second portions of the connection member to alter the overall resistance of the modified elastic resistance band.
claims:
Claims I claim: 1. A resistance band dimensioned to be received around a portion of an associated user, the resistance band comprising: an elongated strap having a proximal, first end and a distal, second end spaced therefrom, the strap formed at least in part of a material that is elastic so that a first length defined along a longitudinal axis between the first end and second end without any stretching force imposed thereon may be increased to a larger, second length greater than the first length along the longitudinal axis between the first end and second end in response to a stretching force imposed thereon; and a connection member having a first portion that selectively connects and disconnects with a second portion of the connection member, the first portion and the second portion of the connection member secured at spaced locations of the elongated strap so that, when disconnected, the strap and connection member may be received around a leg portion of the associated user and, when connected, the strap and connection member form a closed loop resistance band around the leg portion of the associated user that is selectively stretched toward the second length in response to a stretching force applied thereto by the associated user and relaxes toward the first length in response to removal of the stretching force. 2. The resistance band of claim 1 wherein the first portion and the second portion of the connection member are provided at terminal ends of the first end and the second end, respectively, of the strap. 3. The resistance band of claim 1 wherein the first portion of the connection member includes a body having (i) one end secured to the first end of the strap and (ii) an outer, other end including an axially extending recess that operatively communicates with an end opening. 4. The resistance band of claim 3 wherein the body of the first portion of the connection member includes first and second side openings spaced axially from the end opening and the side openings communicate with the recess. 5. The resistance band of claim 4 wherein the second portion of the connection member includes a body having (i) one end secured to the second end of the strap and (ii) other end of the body includes first and second fingers. 6. The resistance band of claim 5 wherein the fingers extend axially outward and away from the second end of the strap. 7. The resistance band of claim 6 where the fingers of the second portion are formed of a material allowing the fingers to selectively flex inwardly toward one another for receipt through the recess of the first portion. 8. The resistance band of claim 7 wherein each of the fingers includes a transverse shoulder located axially inward from a terminal end of the fingers such that upon axial insertion of the fingers that selectively engages an axial end of the respective side openings and thereby preclude axial separation of the first and second portions of the connection member. 9. The resistance band of claim 8 wherein the transverse shoulders of the fingers of the second portion of the connection member have a greater transverse dimension than the end opening in the first portion of the connection member, and the fingers each include tapering regions having a small first dimension that fit within the end opening of the connection member first portion, and the tapering regions deflect the fingers inwardly toward one another upon axial advancement of the tapering regions of the second portion through the end opening in the first portion and when the transverse shoulders reach the side openings in the connection member first portion, the fingers 

flex outwardly through the side openings to complete make-up of the connection member. 10. The resistance band of claim 1 wherein the elastic material of the strap is capable of stretching to at least 110% of its original length. 11. The resistance band of claim 10 wherein the elastic material of the strap is capable of stretching up to a factor of 10 of its original length. 12. A method of exercising with a resistance band comprising the steps of: providing a resistance band having a first length formed by an elongated strap having a proximal, first end and a distal, second end spaced therefrom, the strap formed at least in part of a material that is elastic, and connection member having a first portion that selectively connects and disconnects with a second portion of the connection member; securing the first portion and the second portion of the connection member to form a closed loop resistance band around the leg portion of the associated user; and selectively stretching the resistance band toward a second length greater than the first length in response to a stretching force applied thereto by the associated user and relaxing the resistance band toward the first length in response to removal of the stretching force. 13, The method of claim 12 further comprising disconnecting the first portion from the second portion of the resistance band. 14. The method of claim 12 wherein the stretching includes elongating the resistance band at least 110% and up to a factor of 10 of its original length. 15. A kit comprising: 

 first and second resistance bands each dimensioned to be received around a portion of an associated user, each of the resistance bands including an elongated strap having a proximal, first end and a distal, second end spaced therefrom, the strap formed at least in part of a material that is elastic so that a first length defined along a longitudinal axis between the first end and second end without any stretching force imposed thereon may be increased to a larger, second length greater than the first length along the longitudinal axis between the first end and second end in response to a stretching force imposed thereon; a connection member having a first portion that selectively connects and disconnects with a second portion of the connection member, the first portion and the second portion of the connection member secured at spaced locations of the elongated strap so that, when disconnected, the strap and connection member may be received around a leg portion of the associated user and, when connected, the strap and connection member form a closed loop resistance band around the leg portion of the associated user that is selectively stretched toward the second length in response to a stretching force applied thereto by the associated user and relaxes toward the first length in response to removal of the stretching force; and the elastic material of the first and second straps being different so that an associated user encounters different levels of resistance force.
